# Terraform `terraform test` Framework - Golden Pass Coverage Report

Cross-checked against context7 (`/websites/developer_hashicorp_terraform`) and official `developer.hashicorp.com` docs + `hashicorp/terraform` GitHub CHANGELOGs. Today = 2026-06; **1.15.0 is released, 1.16.0 is unreleased (experimental).**

## Version reality check
- The framework GA'd in **1.6.0** (Oct 2023). Mocking added in **1.7.0** (Feb 2024). Everything else is incremental through 1.11-1.16.
- The earlier web-search claim that "1.15 doesn't exist" is **stale** (model cutoff Jan 2026). 1.15.0 ships and 1.16.0 is the unreleased experimental line.

---

## 1. `.tftest.hcl` file structure

| Feature | What it does | Introduced | Status |
|---|---|---|---|
| Test files `.tftest.hcl` / `.tftest.json` | Discovered by extension; default dir `tests/` | 1.6.0 | Stable |
| `run` block | Simulates a Terraform command in the config dir; sequential by default; 1..N per file | 1.6.0 | Stable |
| `command = plan \| apply` | Selects operation; **default `apply`** | 1.6.0 | Stable |
| File-level `variables` block | 0..1; values passed to all runs | 1.6.0 | Stable |
| Run-level `variables` block | Overrides file-level for that run | 1.6.0 | Stable |
| `module` block (run-level) | Loads an **alternate** module for the run; only `source` + `version` attrs | 1.6.0 | Stable |
| `provider` blocks (file-level) | Configure providers like main config; **can reference test vars + prior run outputs** (`run.vault_setup.aws_access_key`) | 1.6.0 (refs 1.7.0+) | Stable |
| `providers` map (run-level) | Maps provider aliases → test-file providers | 1.6.0 | Stable |

## 2. Assertions & negative paths

| Feature | What it does | Introduced | Status |
|---|---|---|---|
| `assert { condition / error_message }` | Positive-path checks; failures reported at end | 1.6.0 | Stable |
| `expect_failures = [ ... ]` | Negative-path: expects a checkable object to fail. Targets: **variables** (`var.x`), **outputs** (`output.y`), **resources**, **check blocks** | 1.6.0 | Stable |

Caveat (doc-confirmed): custom conditions other than `check` blocks **halt** the plan/apply. Don't mix `expect_failures` (on a variable) with `assert` in the same run expecting the assert to run - the halt skips the assert. Best practice: one or the other per run.

## 3. Mocking & overrides

| Feature | What it does | Introduced | Status |
|---|---|---|---|
| `mock_provider "x" {}` | Fake provider; auto-generates synthetic computed values, no creds/API | 1.7.0 | Stable |
| `mock_resource { type, defaults }` (nested in `mock_provider`) | Override resource computed values; provider not called | 1.7.0 | Stable |
| `mock_data { type, defaults }` | Same for data sources | 1.7.0 | Stable |
| `defaults` object | Per-type returned attribute values inside mock/override | 1.7.0 | Stable |
| `override_resource { target, values }` | Replace specific resource values (file or run level) | 1.7.0 | Stable |
| `override_data { target, values }` | Replace data-source values | 1.7.0 | Stable |
| `override_module { target, outputs }` | Replace a module's outputs (uses `outputs`, not `values`) | 1.7.0 | Stable |
| `*.tfmock.hcl` / `.tfmock.json` files | Reusable `mock_resource`/`mock_data` libraries, loaded via `source` in `mock_provider` | 1.7.0 | Stable |
| `override_during = plan \| apply` | When mocked/overridden values materialize. **Default `apply`** (returns `(known after apply)` at plan). Set `plan` to use values during plan-only unit runs | **1.11.0** (#36227) | Stable |
| Functions inside mock blocks | `defaults` may call Terraform functions | **1.15.0** (#34672) | Stable |

Auto-generated value rules: number→0, bool→false, string→random 8-char alnum, collections→empty, objects→recursive.

## 4. Parallelism

| Feature | What it does | Introduced | Status |
|---|---|---|---|
| `parallel = true` (run/test level) | Run independent run blocks concurrently | **1.12.0** (#34237) | Stable |
| `-parallelism=<n>` CLI | Concurrent plan/apply ops within a run; **default 10** | **1.12.0** | Stable |
| `state_key` (run block) | Controls which internal state file a run uses; lets unrelated runs share state | **1.11.0** (#36185) | Stable |
| Parallel teardown | Teardown ops run in parallel | **1.13.0** (#37169) | Stable |
| Parallelism=1 deadlock fix | - | 1.13.0 (#37292) | Stable |

Two runs go parallel only if: both `parallel=true`, no output dependency, and different state keys (no shared state). Note your earlier guess (1.12/1.13): **`state_key` = 1.11**, **`parallel`/`-parallelism` = 1.12**.

## 5. Run command & plan_options

| Feature | What it does | Introduced | Status |
|---|---|---|---|
| `plan_options { mode }` | `normal` (default) or `refresh-only` | 1.6.0 | Stable |
| `plan_options { refresh }` | bool, default `true` | 1.6.0 | Stable |
| `plan_options { replace = [...] }` | Force-replace resource addresses | 1.6.0 | Stable |
| `plan_options { target = [...] }` | Limit to resource addresses | 1.6.0 | Stable |

## 6. Setup/helper modules & run chaining

| Feature | What it does | Introduced | Status |
|---|---|---|---|
| Setup/helper module pattern | `tests/setup/` modules build prerequisites (e.g. random names); separate from module under test | 1.6.0 | Stable |
| `run.<name>.<output>` references | Later runs consume prior run outputs (e.g. `run.setup_tests.bucket_prefix`) | 1.6.0 | Stable |
| File-level vars referencing run outputs/other vars | File-level `variables` can reference run outputs and other variables | **1.13.0** (#37205) | Stable |
| External variable definitions in test files | `variable` blocks defining externally-referenced vars; **now required** when referenced | **1.13.0** (#37195, upgrade note) | Stable |

## 8. `terraform test` CLI

| Flag | What it does | Introduced | Status |
|---|---|---|---|
| `-filter=<file>` | Limit to specified test file(s) | 1.6.0 | Stable |
| `-verbose` | Print plan/state per run; (1.14: includes expected diagnostics) | 1.6.0 | Stable |
| `-json` | Machine-readable output | 1.6.0 | Stable |
| `-test-directory=<dir>` | Override `tests/` dir | 1.6.0 | Stable |
| `-no-cleanup` | Skip resource destroy after run (confirmed via docs/search) | 1.6.x | Stable |
| `-junit-xml=<path>` | JUnit XML report (incompatible with `-cloud-run`) | **GA in 1.11.0** (#36324) | Stable |
| `-parallelism=<n>` | See §4 | 1.12.0 | Stable |
| `-cloud-run=<module source>` | Run remotely on HCP Terraform | 1.6.x | Stable |
| JUnit skipped-test diagnostics | File-level errors surfaced in JUnit `skipped` elements for CI | **1.15.0** (#37801) | Stable |

## 9. Notable changelog entries by version

- **1.10** - no headline `test` changes (incremental fixes).
- **1.11.0** - `-junit-xml` **GA** (#36324); `override_during = plan` (#36227); **`state_key`** (#36185).
- **1.12.0** - `parallel` annotation + `-parallelism=n` (#34237); continue execution when expected failure not encountered; detailed diagnostic objects on assert failure; `init` allowed when only tests present.
- **1.13.0** (Aug 20 2025) - parallel teardown (#37169); external variable definitions in test files (#37195, **breaking upgrade note**); file-level var blocks reference run outputs/vars (#37205); parallelism=1 deadlock fix.
- **1.14.0** (Nov 19 2025) - expected diagnostics in verbose output (#37362); **ignore `prevent_destroy` during test cleanup** (#37364); console/test explicit diagnostics for resources absent from last op (#37663).
- **1.15.0** - **functions allowed in mock blocks** (#34672); JUnit XML skipped-element diagnostics (#37801); `.tfquery.hcl` fmt support (#38398, query feature, not test).
- **1.16.0 (UNRELEASED / EXPERIMENTAL)**:
  - **`terraform test cleanup`** command - cleans up state files left by interrupted/failed test runs; a manifest + state files for failed cleanups saved in `.terraform/`.
  - **`skip_cleanup`** attribute (test-file and run level) - tells `terraform test` not to destroy/clean state for that run; left in `.terraform/` for manual `test cleanup`.
  - **`backend` blocks inside `run` blocks** - load state from a backend instead of empty state each run; keeps long-running test infra alive between operations.
  - ENHANCEMENT: "Show info when resources are left behind due to `skip_cleanup`."

## 10. Registry / HCP Terraform

- `-cloud-run=app.terraform.io/ORG/module/provider` runs tests **remotely on HCP Terraform** against a **private registry module**; centralizes creds, integrates CI/CD for published modules. Incompatible with `-junit-xml`.

## 11. HashiCorp best practices (tutorial)

- Tests live in `tests/`; helper/setup modules in `tests/setup/`.
- Module repo naming `terraform-<PROVIDER>-<NAME>`; descriptive run-block names.
- `command = plan` for cheap logic/validation checks; `command = apply` to assert real created state.
- Tests act as combined unit + integration; guard against breaking changes.
- Mock expensive resources (EC2/RDS) with `override_resource`/`mock_provider` to speed runs.
- Chain runs via `run.<name>.<output>`; teardown is reverse run order.

---

# GAP CHECKLIST (tick what your demo covers)

**MUST-SHOW GOLDEN (core of a 20-min module-testing demo):**
- [ ] `.tftest.hcl` in `tests/`, `terraform test` invocation
- [ ] `run` block with `command = plan` vs `command = apply`
- [ ] `assert { condition / error_message }` (positive path)
- [ ] File-level vs run-level `variables` (override precedence)
- [ ] `expect_failures = [var.x]` for **variable validation** (negative path) - the canonical "even/odd" example
- [ ] `mock_provider "aws" {}` - run tests with zero cloud cost/creds (THE headline feature)
- [ ] `override_resource` / `override_data` to skip slow resources (EC2/RDS)
- [ ] Setup/helper module + `run.<setup>.<output>` chaining (random bucket name pattern)
- [ ] `-verbose` to show plan/state per run

**STRONG SECONDARY (show if time / "gold" polish):**
- [ ] `mock_resource` / `mock_data` with `defaults` inside `mock_provider`
- [ ] `*.tfmock.hcl` reusable mock library via `source`
- [ ] `override_during = plan` (1.11) - mocked values during plan-only unit runs
- [ ] `plan_options { mode = refresh-only / refresh / replace / target }`
- [ ] `-junit-xml` report for CI (1.11 GA)
- [ ] `expect_failures` against outputs/resources/checks (beyond variables)

**NICHE / ADVANCED (mention, don't dwell):**
- [ ] `parallel = true` + `-parallelism=n` (1.12) and `state_key` (1.11)
- [ ] `module` block alternate-module loading
- [ ] `providers` alias mapping; provider config referencing run outputs
- [ ] Functions inside mock `defaults` (1.15)
- [ ] `-cloud-run` against HCP Terraform / Registry modules
- [ ] External `variable` definitions requirement (1.13 breaking change - worth a caveat slide)

**BLEEDING EDGE / PREVIEW (flag as "coming in 1.16, experimental"):**
- [ ] `terraform test cleanup` command
- [ ] `skip_cleanup` attribute
- [ ] `backend` blocks inside `run` blocks (persistent test state)

## Demo gotcha to teach
Don't combine `expect_failures` on a variable with an `assert` in the same run - the variable validation halts the plan and the assert never evaluates. One pattern per run.

## Key sources
- [Tests - Configuration Language](https://developer.hashicorp.com/terraform/language/tests)
- [Mocking](https://developer.hashicorp.com/terraform/language/tests/mocking)
- [terraform test CLI](https://developer.hashicorp.com/terraform/cli/commands/test)
- [Write Terraform Tests tutorial](https://developer.hashicorp.com/terraform/tutorials/configuration-language/test)
- CHANGELOGs: [1.11.0](https://github.com/hashicorp/terraform/blob/v1.11.0/CHANGELOG.md), [1.12.0](https://github.com/hashicorp/terraform/blob/v1.12.0/CHANGELOG.md), [1.13.0](https://github.com/hashicorp/terraform/blob/v1.13.0/CHANGELOG.md), [1.14.0](https://github.com/hashicorp/terraform/blob/v1.14.0/CHANGELOG.md), [1.15.0](https://github.com/hashicorp/terraform/blob/v1.15.0/CHANGELOG.md), [main/1.16 unreleased](https://github.com/hashicorp/terraform/blob/main/CHANGELOG.md)

One correction to flag for your lab notes: **`state_key` is 1.11, not 1.12/1.13**, and **`parallel`/`-parallelism` is 1.12**. Mocking itself is 1.7 (not a 1.1x feature). 1.16's cleanup/skip_cleanup/backend-in-run trio is still **experimental and unreleased** as of now - demo it only on an experimental build with a caveat.

  - **`backend` blocks inside `run` blocks** - load state from a backend instead of empty state each run; keeps long-running test infra alive between operations.
  - ENHANCEMENT: "Show info when resources are left behind due to `skip_cleanup`."

## 10. Registry / HCP Terraform

- `-cloud-run=app.terraform.io/ORG/module/provider` runs tests **remotely on HCP Terraform** against a **private registry module**; centralizes creds, integrates CI/CD for published modules. Incompatible with `-junit-xml`.

## 11. HashiCorp best practices (tutorial)

- Tests live in `tests/`; helper/setup modules in `tests/setup/`.
- Module repo naming `terraform-<PROVIDER>-<NAME>`; descriptive run-block names.
- `command = plan` for cheap logic/validation checks; `command = apply` to assert real created state.
- Tests act as combined unit + integration; guard against breaking changes.
- Mock expensive resources (EC2/RDS) with `override_resource`/`mock_provider` to speed runs.
- Chain runs via `run.<name>.<output>`; teardown is reverse run order.

---

# GAP CHECKLIST (tick what your demo covers)

**MUST-SHOW GOLDEN (core of a 20-min module-testing demo):**
- [ ] `.tftest.hcl` in `tests/`, `terraform test` invocation
- [ ] `run` block with `command = plan` vs `command = apply`
- [ ] `assert { condition / error_message }` (positive path)
- [ ] File-level vs run-level `variables` (override precedence)
- [ ] `expect_failures = [var.x]` for **variable validation** (negative path) - the canonical "even/odd" example
- [ ] `mock_provider "aws" {}` - run tests with zero cloud cost/creds (THE headline feature)
- [ ] `override_resource` / `override_data` to skip slow resources (EC2/RDS)
- [ ] Setup/helper module + `run.<setup>.<output>` chaining (random bucket name pattern)
- [ ] `-verbose` to show plan/state per run

**STRONG SECONDARY (show if time / "gold" polish):**
- [ ] `mock_resource` / `mock_data` with `defaults` inside `mock_provider`
- [ ] `*.tfmock.hcl` reusable mock library via `source`
- [ ] `override_during = plan` (1.11) - mocked values during plan-only unit runs
- [ ] `plan_options { mode = refresh-only / refresh / replace / target }`
- [ ] `-junit-xml` report for CI (1.11 GA)
- [ ] `expect_failures` against outputs/resources/checks (beyond variables)

**NICHE / ADVANCED (mention, don't dwell):**
- [ ] `parallel = true` + `-parallelism=n` (1.12) and `state_key` (1.11)
- [ ] `module` block alternate-module loading
- [ ] `providers` alias mapping; provider config referencing run outputs
- [ ] Functions inside mock `defaults` (1.15)
- [ ] `-cloud-run` against HCP Terraform / Registry modules
- [ ] External `variable` definitions requirement (1.13 breaking change - worth a caveat slide)

**BLEEDING EDGE / PREVIEW (flag as "coming in 1.16, experimental"):**
- [ ] `terraform test cleanup` command
- [ ] `skip_cleanup` attribute
- [ ] `backend` blocks inside `run` blocks (persistent test state)

## Demo gotcha to teach
Don't combine `expect_failures` on a variable with an `assert` in the same run - the variable validation halts the plan and the assert never evaluates. One pattern per run.

## Key sources
- [Tests - Configuration Language](https://developer.hashicorp.com/terraform/language/tests)
- [Mocking](https://developer.hashicorp.com/terraform/language/tests/mocking)
- [terraform test CLI](https://developer.hashicorp.com/terraform/cli/commands/test)
- [Write Terraform Tests tutorial](https://developer.hashicorp.com/terraform/tutorials/configuration-language/test)
- CHANGELOGs: [1.11.0](https://github.com/hashicorp/terraform/blob/v1.11.0/CHANGELOG.md), [1.12.0](https://github.com/hashicorp/terraform/blob/v1.12.0/CHANGELOG.md), [1.13.0](https://github.com/hashicorp/terraform/blob/v1.13.0/CHANGELOG.md), [1.14.0](https://github.com/hashicorp/terraform/blob/v1.14.0/CHANGELOG.md), [1.15.0](https://github.com/hashicorp/terraform/blob/v1.15.0/CHANGELOG.md), [main/1.16 unreleased](https://github.com/hashicorp/terraform/blob/main/CHANGELOG.md)

One correction to flag for your lab notes: **`state_key` is 1.11, not 1.12/1.13**, and **`parallel`/`-parallelism` is 1.12**. Mocking itself is 1.7 (not a 1.1x feature). 1.16's cleanup/skip_cleanup/backend-in-run trio is still **experimental and unreleased** as of now - demo it only on an experimental build with a caveat.
CI config lue. Une question clé pour le plan: la couche creds-free tourne-t-elle réellement en CI? Je vérifie le workflow terraform.

file.content:
name: Terraform quality

on:
  push:
    branches: ['**']
  pull_request:
    branches: [main]

concurrency:
  group: terraform-${{ github.ref }}
  cancel-in-progress: true

permissions:
  contents: read

jobs:
  discover:
    name: Discover Terraform root modules
    # Always run on push; on pull_request, run only for fork PRs
    # (same-repo PRs are already covered by the push event).
    if: github.event_name == 'push' || github.event.pull_request.head.repo.full_name != github.repository
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    outputs:
      cells: ${{ steps.find.outputs.cells }}
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@df4cb1c069e1874edd31b4311f1884172cec0e10 # v6.0.3
      - id: find
        # Any directory under examples/ containing providers.tf is a root module to validate.
        # name = path minus examples/ ; example = examples/<name> (holds the per-example tooling).
        run: |
          cells=$(find examples -name providers.tf -printf '%h\n' | sort \
            | jq -R -s '
                split("\n")
                | map(select(length > 0))
                | map({
                    dir: .,
                    name: (sub("^examples/"; "")),
                    example: (split("/")[0:2] | join("/"))
                  })')
          echo "cells=$(echo "$cells" | jq -c .)" >> "$GITHUB_OUTPUT"

  fmt:
    name: terraform fmt
    needs: discover
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@df4cb1c069e1874edd31b4311f1884172cec0e10 # v6.0.3
      - uses: ./.github/actions/tf-setup
        with:
          install-tflint: 'false'
      - run: terraform fmt -check -recursive -diff

  quality:
    name: validate + lint + scan (${{ matrix.cell.name }})
    needs: discover
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    strategy:
      fail-fast: false
      matrix:
        cell: ${{ fromJSON(needs.discover.outputs.cells) }}
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@df4cb1c069e1874edd31b4311f1884172cec0e10 # v6.0.3

      - uses: ./.github/actions/tf-setup
        with:
          working-directory: ${{ matrix.cell.dir }}

      - name: terraform init (no backend)
        working-directory: ${{ matrix.cell.dir }}
        run: terraform init -backend=false -input=false

      - name: terraform validate
        working-directory: ${{ matrix.cell.dir }}
        run: terraform validate -no-color

      - name: tflint init
        working-directory: ${{ matrix.cell.dir }}
        run: tflint --init --config "${{ github.workspace }}/${{ matrix.cell.example }}/.tflint.hcl"
        env:
          GITHUB_TOKEN: ${{ secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN }}

      - name: tflint
        working-directory: ${{ matrix.cell.dir }}
        run: tflint --recursive --format compact --config "${{ github.workspace }}/${{ matrix.cell.example }}/.tflint.hcl"

      - name: install trivy
        uses: aquasecurity/setup-trivy@81e514348e19b6112ce2a7e3ecbafe19c1e1f567 # v0.3.1
        with:
          # Pinned for reproducible CI and bumped by Renovate (annotation below).
          # v0.71.1 once failed to install via setup-trivy's get.trivy.dev path, so
          # CI gates any bump - keep an eye on the Renovate PR's checks.
          # renovate: datasource=github-releases depName=aquasecurity/trivy
          version: v0.71.0
          cache: true

      - name: trivy config scan (report + gate)
        working-directory: ${{ matrix.cell.dir }}
        run: |
          # Scan once to JSON, then convert the same results twice:
          #   - report: MEDIUM,HIGH,CRITICAL, never fails the job (visibility).
          #   - gate:   HIGH,CRITICAL, fails the job (exit-code 1) if any are found.
          trivy config . --format json --output trivy.json --exit-code 0

          echo "::group::Trivy findings (MEDIUM,HIGH,CRITICAL - report only)"
          trivy convert --format table --severity MEDIUM,HIGH,CRITICAL --exit-code 0 trivy.json
          echo "::endgroup::"

          trivy convert --format table --severity HIGH,CRITICAL --exit-code 1 trivy.json

input.command: cat .github/workflows/pr-title.yml .github/workflows/release.yml release-please-config.json 2>/dev/null && echo "===CHANGELOG head===" && head -20 CHANGELOG.md

content.text:
Analyse complète. Synthèse ci-dessous, puis la question de priorisation.

⚠️ **Contrainte temporelle**: live demain (2026-06-25, J-1). Code + slides déjà faits (PR #33). Tout ce qui suit est polish/amélioration, pas du blocage.

## 1. Prévu (conception éditoriale) vs Présent (repo)

| # | Élément prévu (pièce/idée) | Type | Présent dans le repo ? |
|---|---|---|---|
| L1 | Module S3 child + exemple root | live-code | ✅ `modules/s3-bucket/` + `main.tf` |
| L2 | Validation simple + `expect_failures` rouge→vert | live-code | 🟡 Tests ✅ ; **geste rouge→vert non documenté** dans le repo |
| L3 | Validations croisées (1.9+) chiffrement⇒KMS, prod⇒¬force_destroy | live-code | ✅ `variables.tf` + 2 runs |
| L4 | KMS conditionnel `count` (continuité deferred actions) | live-code | ✅ `main.tf` locals |
| L5 | Test plan + `mock_provider`, creds-free | live-code | ✅ `plan.tftest.hcl` |
| L6 | Apply réel sandbox + assertions + auto-destroy | live-code | ✅ `deploy.tftest.hcl` |
| L7 | `parallel` + `state_key`, 2 déploiements | live-code | ✅ `parallel.tftest.hcl` |
| S1 | **CI gate : couche mock tourne sans secret** | slide | ❌ **README l'affirme, mais `terraform.yml` ne lance PAS `terraform test`** |
| S2 | Cleanup crash : `default_tags` + sweeper + teaser 1.16 | slide | ✅ `sweep.sh` + `providers.tf` + README |
| I1-I4, S3 | Slides (pyramide, comparatif outils, layering, CTA) | slide | ➖ Assets éditoriaux (hors repo, normal) |
| - | Structure **démo progressive en stages** (pyramide→stages) | conception | 🟡 Makefile a les cibles, **README est une référence plate, pas un walkthrough staged** |

**2 vrais gaps**: S1 (CI ne joue pas les tests creds-free, contredit le README + la slide) et la **structure progressive** (le README ne raconte pas les étapes de la pyramide).

## 2. Golden pass `terraform test` (couverture vs doc 1.6→1.16)

Couvert (golden core): `.tftest.hcl`, `run`/`command plan|apply`, `assert`, `expect_failures` (variables), `mock_provider`, setup module + `run.x.output`, file-level vs run-level `variables`, `parallel`+`state_key`. **Très bonne base.**

Manquant, par valeur pour ce live:
- 🥇 `-junit-xml` (GA 1.11) — **rapport CI**, sert directement la narrative S1 "CI gate"
- 🥇 Câbler la couche creds-free dans la CI (ferme le gap S1)
- 🥈 `override_resource`/`mock_resource { defaults }` — profondeur du mocking au-delà du `mock_provider {}` nu
- 🥈 **Functions in mock `defaults` (nouveauté 1.15)** — un highlight "version récente" gratuit
- 🥉 `override_during = plan` (1.11), `plan_options` (refresh-only/replace/target), `*.tfmock.hcl`, `expect_failures` sur outputs/checks — niche
- 🔮 1.16 preview `skip_cleanup` / `test cleanup` — déjà teasé au README ✅

Correction doc à porter: commentaire `parallel.tftest.hcl:2` dit `state_key (>= 1.12)` — en fait **`state_key` = 1.11**, `parallel` = 1.12.

## 3. Cohérence langue — violation confirmée

Tout l'exemple est en **FR**, le reste du repo en **EN** (root AGENTS.md, README, autres exemples). À traduire: `README.md`, `AGENTS.md`, commentaires de `Makefile` + 4 `.tftest.hcl` + `main.tf`/`variables.tf`/`providers.tf`/`setup/main.tf`, et commentaires **+ strings d'output FR** de `sweep.sh`. Les identifiants sont déjà EN.

**Cause racine**: aucun AGENTS.md ne stipule explicitement "anglais partout" — la convention est seulement implicite par mimétisme. D'où le dérapage.

## 4. Plan de mise à jour AGENTS.md (conventions)

| Convention | État actuel | Proposition |
|---|---|---|
| **Langue EN partout** (code/commentaires/docs) | implicite seulement → a laissé passer le FR | **Ajouter une règle explicite** au root AGENTS.md (anti-récurrence #1) |
| Conventional Commits | ✅ documenté | Mentionner les gates `commitlint.yml` + `pr-title.yml` (semantic PR title) |
| Semantic release | ✅ release-please documenté | RAS |
| CI lint (fmt/tflint/trivy) | ✅ documenté | RAS |
| **Pre-commit** | ❌ absent | **Proposer** `.pre-commit-config.yaml` (fmt/tflint/validate locaux, miroir CI) — process à mettre en place |
| `terraform test` en CI | ❌ non câblé | Documenter une fois câblé (cf. gap S1) |

## 5. Chantiers de travail

- **A — Démo progressive** : réécrire le README en stages mappés à la pyramide (Stage 0 présenter module/structure → 1 validations → 2 plan/mock creds-free → 3 deploy intégration → 4 parallel), chaque stage = couche + cible make + ce qu'il prouve + geste rouge→vert documenté. Cibles Makefile atomiques par stage si besoin.
- **B — Langue FR→EN** : traduire les 11 fichiers ci-dessus.
- **C — AGENTS.md conventions** : règle langue EN + gates commit + (décision) pre-commit.
- **D — Golden features** : câbler couche creds-free + `-junit-xml` en CI (ferme S1), mini-démo `override_resource`, highlight functions-in-mock 1.15, fix versions.

options.label: A → B → C (D post-live)
options.description: Recommandé J-1 : la démo progressive sert directement le live, la langue EN est un quick-win (repo à l'écran + public), conventions ensuite. Golden features (D) en stretch après le live.

# Demo run-of-show - live "Terraform test" (2026-06-25).
# terraform 1.15.6 (see .terraform-version). The real apply targets the sandbox AWS profile.
# Targets are ordered as the progressive demo stages (read the README "Progressive demo").
TF := terraform

.PHONY: init fmt validate test-validate test-plan test-fast test-deploy test-parallel test sweep clean

init:           ## init (providers: aws + random)
	$(TF) init

fmt:            ## format check
	$(TF) fmt -recursive -check -diff

validate:       ## validate the config
	$(TF) validate

test-validate:  ## Stage 1 - variable validations only (expect_failures, creds-free)
	$(TF) test -filter=tests/validations.tftest.hcl -verbose

test-plan:      ## Stage 2 - mocked plan assertions only (mock_provider, creds-free)
	$(TF) test -filter=tests/plan.tftest.hcl -verbose

test-fast:      ## Stages 1+2 - creds-free layer (runs in CI without secrets)
	$(TF) test -filter=tests/validations.tftest.hcl -filter=tests/plan.tftest.hcl -verbose

test-deploy:    ## Stage 3 - integration: real apply on AWS (requires AWS_PROFILE=sandbox)
	AWS_PROFILE=sandbox $(TF) test -filter=tests/deploy.tftest.hcl

test-parallel:  ## Stage 4 - parallel + state_key: 2 concurrent deployments (AWS_PROFILE=sandbox)
	AWS_PROFILE=sandbox $(TF) test -filter=tests/parallel.tftest.hcl

test:           ## full suite (AWS_PROFILE=sandbox for the apply stages)
	AWS_PROFILE=sandbox $(TF) test

sweep:          ## clean up orphaned resources by tag (see sweep.sh)
	./sweep.sh

clean:          ## remove local terraform state
	find . -type d -name '.terraform' -prune -exec rm -rf {} + ; find . -name '*.tfstate*' -delete

input.content:
# terraform-module-testing

> **type** `lab` · **tags** `aws` `terraform-test` `testing` `s3` `validation` `mock` `parallel`

Test a Terraform module with the native `terraform test` framework, as a **progressive live demo**: validate the inputs, break it on purpose, plan it without the cloud, then deploy it for real and assert the resources. Companion to the "Terraform test" live (2026-06-25). No Go, pure HCL.

## The test pyramid (rather, a diamond)

Native `terraform test` covers the bottom and middle of the pyramid; the top (e2e, polling, retries) stays out of scope and belongs to Terratest. The stages below climb that pyramid, each demo stage maps to one layer:

| Pyramid layer | Stage | What it proves | Cloud / creds | `make` target |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| Static (lint/scan) | - | syntax, security, policy | none | `fmt` `validate` (+ CI tflint/trivy) |
| Unit-ish: validation | **1** | inputs fail fast (`expect_failures`) | none (mock) | `test-validate` |
| Unit-ish: plan | **2** | variables wire into the planned config (`mock_provider`) | none (mock) | `test-plan` |
| Integration | **3** | real apply, resources assert, auto-destroy | yes (sandbox) | `test-deploy` |
| Integration: scale | **4** | `parallel` + `state_key`, concurrent deploys | yes (sandbox) | `test-parallel` |
| E2e / polling | - | out of native scope → Terratest | - | - |

The first two stages run **without credentials** (CI without secrets). The last two deploy on a real account (`AWS_PROFILE=sandbox`) and `terraform test` destroys at the end of each file.

## Stage 0 - read the module (no test yet)

`modules/s3-bucket/` creates an S3 bucket with versioning, public-access blocking and conditional SSE-KMS encryption. It encodes decisions as guardrails:

- **simple validations**: bucket name (S3 rules), `environment` (dev/staging/prod);
- **cross-variable validations** (Terraform 1.9+): `enable_encryption` requires `kms_key_arn`; `prod` forbids `force_destroy`;
- **conditional encryption**: `count = var.kms_key_arn != null ? 1 : 0` (the decision is driven by the ARN value).

The root (`main.tf` + `providers.tf`) is an example usage and the configuration under test. Open these first: the point is that a module is decisions you can prove.

## Stage 1 - validations (fail fast, creds-free)

`tests/validations.tftest.hcl` - one atomic `run` per validator, `command = plan`, `expect_failures` against each `var.*`. The validation halts before any provider call, so the layer is credential-free.

```bash
make test-validate              # 5 runs green, no AWS_PROFILE
```

**Red → green demo**: comment out the cross-validation on `enable_encryption` in `modules/s3-bucket/variables.tf`, rerun: the `encryption_requires_kms_key` run goes red (its `expect_failures` is no longer satisfied). Restore the validation, rerun: green again. The test pins the guardrail.

## Stage 2 - plan with `mock_provider` (creds-free)

`tests/plan.tftest.hcl` - `mock_provider "aws" {}` plus a file-level `variables {}` block that sets defaults for every `run`. Assertions on the planned config: versioning defaults to `Enabled`, all four public-access-block flags are true, the encryption block is present/absent and the KMS ARN propagates.

```bash
make test-plan                  # 4 runs green in seconds, still no credentials
make test-fast                  # stages 1+2 combined (the CI layer)
```

## Stage 3 - real apply (integration)

`tests/deploy.tftest.hcl` - `command = apply` on a real account. A `setup` helper module mints a random suffix for a globally unique bucket name; assertions run against the real outputs; `terraform test` destroys everything at the end of the file.

```bash
AWS_PROFILE=sandbox make test-deploy
```

## Stage 4 - parallel runs (`state_key`)

`tests/parallel.tftest.hcl` - the same example deployed twice concurrently. `parallel = true` (Terraform 1.12+) plus a distinct `state_key` (1.11+) isolate the state of each run, so they run at the same time without collision.

```bash
AWS_PROFILE=sandbox make test-parallel
AWS_PROFILE=sandbox make test    # the full 14-run suite (apply + auto-destroy)
```

## Cleanup on crash

If `terraform test` is interrupted before its auto-destroy, the in-memory state is lost and the resources leak. Every resource carries a suite tag (`default_tags`), so a sweeper can find them:

```bash
./sweep.sh            # list tagged buckets (dry-run)
./sweep.sh --force    # empty + delete
```

Native `skip_cleanup` + `terraform test cleanup` land as a preview in Terraform 1.16.

## Beyond native testing

Layer the tools, lightest first: static (`tflint`/`trivy`, every commit) → `terraform test` (unit mock + integration apply, this lab) → Terratest when e2e/polling is required. `terraform test` returns an exit code, so stages 1+2 gate a merge in CI without any secret.

toolUseResult.originalFile:
# terraform-module-testing

> **type** `lab` · **tags** `aws` `terraform-test` `testing` `s3` `validation` `mock` `parallel`

Tester un module Terraform avec le framework natif `terraform test` : valider les entrées, le casser exprès, le déployer pour de vrai. Support du live "Terraform test" (2026-06-25).

## Le module

`modules/s3-bucket/` crée un bucket S3 avec versioning, blocage des accès publics et chiffrement SSE-KMS conditionnel. Il porte des garde-fous :

- **validations simples** : nom de bucket (règles S3), `environment` (dev/staging/prod) ;
- **validations croisées** (Terraform 1.9+) : `enable_encryption` exige `kms_key_arn` ; `prod` interdit `force_destroy` ;
- **chiffrement conditionnel** : `count = var.kms_key_arn != null ? 1 : 0` (la décision se prend sur la valeur de l'ARN).

## Les tests

| Fichier | command | Cloud | Couvre |
|---|---|---|---|
| `tests/validations.tftest.hcl` | plan | non (mock) | `expect_failures` sur chaque validateur |
| `tests/plan.tftest.hcl` | plan | non (mock) | toutes les variables câblées dans le plan |
| `tests/deploy.tftest.hcl` | apply | oui (sandbox) | déploiement réel + assertions ressources |
| `tests/parallel.tftest.hcl` | apply | oui (sandbox) | `parallel` + `state_key`, 2 déploiements concurrents |

Les deux premières couches tournent **sans credentials** (CI sans secret). Les deux dernières déploient sur un vrai compte (`AWS_PROFILE=sandbox`) et `terraform test` détruit en fin de fichier.

## Lancer

```bash
make init
make test-fast                  # validations + plan mocké (creds-free)
AWS_PROFILE=sandbox make test    # suite complète (apply réel + auto-destroy)
```

## Nettoyage sur crash

Si `terraform test` est interrompu avant son auto-destroy, l'état en mémoire disparaît et les ressources fuient. Chaque ressource porte un tag de suite (`default_tags`), donc un sweeper les retrouve :

```bash
./sweep.sh            # liste les buckets taggés (dry-run)
./sweep.sh --force    # vide + supprime
```

Le natif `skip_cleanup` + `terraform test cleanup` arrive en preview Terraform 1.16.

#!/usr/bin/env bash
# Sweeper : nettoie les buckets de test orphelins (laissés par un `terraform test`
# interrompu avant son auto-destroy). Cible les ressources portant le tag de suite.
# Source de vérité du tag : providers.tf (default_tags tftest-suite).
#
# Usage:
#   ./sweep.sh           # liste les buckets taggés (dry-run)
#   ./sweep.sh --force   # vide puis supprime chaque bucket taggé
#
# Requiert AWS_PROFILE (ex: sandbox). Alternative outillée : aws-nuke / awsweeper
# filtrés sur le même tag.
set -euo pipefail

TAG_KEY="tftest-suite"
TAG_VALUE="terraform-module-testing"
REGION="${AWS_REGION:-eu-west-1}"
FORCE="${1:-}"

mapfile -t ARNS < <(aws resourcegroupstaggingapi get-resources \
  --region "$REGION" \
  --tag-filters "Key=${TAG_KEY},Values=${TAG_VALUE}" \
  --resource-type-filters "s3" \
  --query 'ResourceTagMappingList[].ResourceARN' --output text | tr '\t' '\n')

if [ "${#ARNS[@]}" -eq 0 ] || [ -z "${ARNS[0]:-}" ]; then
  echo "Aucun bucket taggé ${TAG_KEY}=${TAG_VALUE}."
  exit 0
fi

echo "Buckets taggés trouvés :"
printf '  %s\n' "${ARNS[@]}"

if [ "$FORCE" != "--force" ]; then
  echo "Dry-run. Relancer avec --force pour vider + supprimer."
  exit 0
fi

for arn in "${ARNS[@]}"; do
  bucket="${arn##*:::}"
  echo "Suppression de ${bucket} ..."
  aws s3 rm "s3://${bucket}" --recursive >/dev/null 2>&1 || true
  aws s3api delete-bucket --bucket "${bucket}" --region "$REGION"
done
echo "Nettoyage terminé."

toolUseResult.originalFile:
#!/usr/bin/env bash
# Sweeper: cleans up orphaned test buckets (left by a `terraform test` interrupted
# before its auto-destroy). Targets resources carrying the suite tag.
# Tag source of truth: providers.tf (default_tags tftest-suite).
#
# Usage:
#   ./sweep.sh           # list tagged buckets (dry-run)
#   ./sweep.sh --force   # empty then delete each tagged bucket
#
# Requires AWS_PROFILE (e.g. sandbox). Tooled alternative: aws-nuke / awsweeper
# filtered on the same tag.
set -euo pipefail

TAG_KEY="tftest-suite"
TAG_VALUE="terraform-module-testing"
REGION="${AWS_REGION:-eu-west-1}"
FORCE="${1:-}"

mapfile -t ARNS < <(aws resourcegroupstaggingapi get-resources \
  --region "$REGION" \
  --tag-filters "Key=${TAG_KEY},Values=${TAG_VALUE}" \
  --resource-type-filters "s3" \
  --query 'ResourceTagMappingList[].ResourceARN' --output text | tr '\t' '\n')

if [ "${#ARNS[@]}" -eq 0 ] || [ -z "${ARNS[0]:-}" ]; then
  echo "No bucket tagged ${TAG_KEY}=${TAG_VALUE}."
  exit 0
fi

echo "Tagged buckets found:"
printf '  %s\n' "${ARNS[@]}"

if [ "$FORCE" != "--force" ]; then
  echo "Dry-run. Re-run with --force to empty + delete."
  exit 0
fi

for arn in "${ARNS[@]}"; do
  bucket="${arn##*:::}"
  echo "Suppression de ${bucket} ..."
  aws s3 rm "s3://${bucket}" --recursive >/dev/null 2>&1 || true
  aws s3api delete-bucket --bucket "${bucket}" --region "$REGION"
done
echo "Nettoyage terminé."

input.content:
# AGENTS.md - terraform-module-testing

Example-specific guidance. Repo-wide conventions: root AGENTS.md.

Taxonomy: **type lab** - live support, progressive stages. Tags: aws, terraform-test, testing, s3, validation, mock, parallel.

## Purpose and scope

Demonstrate the native `terraform test` framework on a reusable S3 module: validate inputs, fail cleanly (`expect_failures`), test the plan without the cloud (`mock_provider`), deploy for real and assert resources, parallelize with `state_key`. Pedagogical, not a production library.

## Architecture

- `modules/s3-bucket/`: the module under test (child). Simple validations (S3 name, environment) + cross-variable ones (encryption ⇒ KMS ARN; prod ⇒ no `force_destroy`). Conditional KMS encryption (`count` on the ARN).
- root (`main.tf` + `providers.tf`): example usage (root) + per-run `default_tags` for the sweeper.
- `tests/`:
  - `validations.tftest.hcl`: `expect_failures`, plan-only, creds-free (mock).
  - `plan.tftest.hcl`: `mock_provider`, assertions on the planned config, creds-free.
  - `deploy.tftest.hcl`: real apply (AWS_PROFILE=sandbox), resource assertions, auto-destroy.
  - `parallel.tftest.hcl`: `parallel` + `state_key`, 2 concurrent deployments.
  - `setup/`: helper module (random suffix for unique names).

## Progressive demo

The README is structured as a progressive live demo climbing the test pyramid: Stage 0 (read the module) → Stage 1 validations → Stage 2 mocked plan → Stage 3 real apply → Stage 4 parallel. Each stage maps to a pyramid layer and a `make` target. Keep that mapping in sync when editing the README, the `Makefile` targets, or the test files.

## Common commands

```bash
make init
make test-validate              # Stage 1 - validations only (creds-free)
make test-plan                  # Stage 2 - mocked plan only (creds-free)
make test-fast                  # Stages 1+2 (CI without secret)
AWS_PROFILE=sandbox make test    # full suite, real apply
./sweep.sh                      # clean orphans by tag (dry-run)
```

## Conventions

- Single-type variables rather than complex objects: isolates each validator's error message.
- One atomic `run` per validator (easy to read, cheap plan-only).
- S3 `versioning` = `Enabled` / `Suspended` (never `Disabled`).

Pédagogique, pas une lib de prod.

## Architecture

- `modules/s3-bucket/` : le module sous test (child). Validations simples (nom S3, environnement) + croisées (chiffrement ⇒ ARN KMS ; prod ⇒ pas de `force_destroy`). Chiffrement KMS conditionnel (`count` sur l'ARN).

  - `setup/` : module helper (suffixe aléatoire pour noms uniques).

# AGENTS.md

Guidance for AI coding agents (Claude Code, Cursor, OpenAI Codex, Aider, etc.) working in this repository. A `CLAUDE.md` symlink points here so Claude Code picks it up automatically. Each example also has its own nested `AGENTS.md` with example-specific guidance - read it before editing inside an example.

## What this repo is

A **library of self-contained Terraform examples**. Each lives under `examples/<name>/` and is independent: its own `README.md`, `AGENTS.md`, tooling (`.terraform-version`, `.tflint.hcl`) and Terraform root module(s). Examples are **not** factored into shared code - isolation is intentional so each can be read, run, and live-demoed on its own.

Use cases served: reusable patterns, illustrations for blog/LinkedIn posts, and starting points / labs for live tech sessions.

## Taxonomy

Every example declares a primary `type` plus free-form tags. Source of truth is the catalogue table in the root `README.md`; each example's `README.md`/`AGENTS.md` restates its type.

- `pattern` - reusable, production-leaning reference.
- `lab` - starting point / TP with progressive steps for a live session.
- `experiment` - preview/RC features, may be intentionally unstable.

Do **not** create top-level category directories (`patterns/`, `experiments/`...). Keep `examples/` flat and classify via `type` + tags. This avoids lifecycle churn when an example changes category.

## How to add an example (golden path)

1. `mkdir examples/<name>/` - kebab-case, scope-first (`aws-multi-env`, `terraform-rc-variables`).
2. Add `README.md` (state its `type` + tags, objective, run steps) and `AGENTS.md` (example-specific guidance).
3. Add per-example tooling: `.terraform-version` (pin, may be an RC/prerelease for `experiment` types) and `.tflint.hcl`.
4. Add the Terraform root module(s). Any directory containing a `providers.tf` is auto-discovered by CI - no CI edit needed to validate it.
5. Register a row in the root `README.md` catalogue table.

## CI

`.github/workflows/terraform.yml` auto-discovers Terraform root modules (directories containing `providers.tf`) under `examples/` and runs `validate` + `tflint` + `trivy` per module in a matrix. `terraform fmt -check -recursive` runs once at the root. The `tf-setup` composite action installs Terraform from the target example's `.terraform-version`. Adding an example requires no CI change.

## Repository standards

- **Git flow**: GitHub flow (short-lived feature branches off `main`, PR review, squash-merge).
- **Work tracking**: durable backlog = GitHub Issues + Milestone (a goal + its acceptance criteria); each PR references its issue (`Closes #N`). `main` carries no task file - session plans stay local/ephemeral. `tasks/lessons.md` holds durable repo lessons.
- **Commit messages**: [Conventional Commits](https://www.conventionalcommits.org/) - `<type>(<scope>): <subject>`. Use the example name as scope when relevant (e.g. `feat(aws-multi-env): ...`).
  - Common types: `feat`, `fix`, `docs`, `chore`, `refactor`, `test`, `ci`, `build`.
  - Breaking changes: append `!` after type/scope or add a `BREAKING CHANGE:` footer.
  - Keep subject imperative, lowercase, no trailing period, ~72 chars max.
- **Versioning**: [SemVer](https://semver.org/), repo-level single release line via release-please.
- **Validation before commit**: `terraform fmt -recursive` (root) and `terraform validate` inside each touched root module.

toolUseResult.originalFile:
# terraform-module-testing

> **type** `lab` · **tags** `aws` `terraform-test` `testing` `s3` `validation` `mock` `parallel`

Test a Terraform module with the native `terraform test` framework, as a **progressive live demo**: validate the inputs, break it on purpose, plan it without the cloud, then deploy it for real and assert the resources. Companion to the "Terraform test" live (2026-06-25). No Go, pure HCL.

## The test pyramid (rather, a diamond)

Native `terraform test` covers the bottom and middle of the pyramid; the top (e2e, polling, retries) stays out of scope and belongs to Terratest. The stages below climb that pyramid, each demo stage maps to one layer:

| Pyramid layer | Stage | What it proves | Cloud / creds | `make` target |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| Static (lint/scan) | - | syntax, security, policy | none | `fmt` `validate` (+ CI tflint/trivy) |
| Unit-ish: validation | **1** | inputs fail fast (`expect_failures`) | none (mock) | `test-validate` |
| Unit-ish: plan | **2** | variables wire into the planned config (`mock_provider`) | none (mock) | `test-plan` |
| Integration | **3** | real apply, resources assert, auto-destroy | yes (sandbox) | `test-deploy` |
| Integration: scale | **4** | `parallel` + `state_key`, concurrent deploys | yes (sandbox) | `test-parallel` |
| E2e / polling | - | out of native scope → Terratest | - | - |

The first two stages run **without credentials** (CI without secrets). The last two deploy on a real account (`AWS_PROFILE=sandbox`) and `terraform test` destroys at the end of each file.

## Stage 0 - read the module (no test yet)

`modules/s3-bucket/` creates an S3 bucket with versioning, public-access blocking and conditional SSE-KMS encryption. It encodes decisions as guardrails:

- **simple validations**: bucket name (S3 rules), `environment` (dev/staging/prod);
- **cross-variable validations** (Terraform 1.9+): `enable_encryption` requires `kms_key_arn`; `prod` forbids `force_destroy`;
- **conditional encryption**: `count = var.kms_key_arn != null ? 1 : 0` (the decision is driven by the ARN value).

The root (`main.tf` + `providers.tf`) is an example usage and the configuration under test. Open these first: the point is that a module is decisions you can prove.

## Stage 1 - validations (fail fast, creds-free)

`tests/validations.tftest.hcl` - one atomic `run` per validator, `command = plan`, `expect_failures` against each `var.*`. The validation halts before any provider call, so the layer is credential-free.

```bash
make test-validate              # 5 runs green, no AWS_PROFILE
```

**Red → green demo**: comment out the cross-validation on `enable_encryption` in `modules/s3-bucket/variables.tf`, rerun: the `encryption_requires_kms_key` run goes red (its `expect_failures` is no longer satisfied). Restore the validation, rerun: green again. The test pins the guardrail.

## Stage 2 - plan with `mock_provider` (creds-free)

`tests/plan.tftest.hcl` - `mock_provider "aws" {}` plus a file-level `variables {}` block that sets defaults for every `run`. Assertions on the planned config: versioning defaults to `Enabled`, all four public-access-block flags are true, the encryption block is present/absent and the KMS ARN propagates.

```bash
make test-plan                  # 4 runs green in seconds, still no credentials
make test-fast                  # stages 1+2 combined (the CI layer)
```

## Stage 3 - real apply (integration)

`tests/deploy.tftest.hcl` - `command = apply` on a real account. A `setup` helper module mints a random suffix for a globally unique bucket name; assertions run against the real outputs; `terraform test` destroys everything at the end of the file.

```bash
AWS_PROFILE=sandbox make test-deploy
```

## Stage 4 - parallel runs (`state_key`)

`tests/parallel.tftest.hcl` - the same example deployed twice concurrently. `parallel = true` (Terraform 1.12+) plus a distinct `state_key` (1.11+) isolate the state of each run, so they run at the same time without collision.

```bash
AWS_PROFILE=sandbox make test-parallel
AWS_PROFILE=sandbox make test    # the full 14-run suite (apply + auto-destroy)
```

## Cleanup on crash

If `terraform test` is interrupted before its auto-destroy, the in-memory state is lost and the resources leak. Every resource carries a suite tag (`default_tags`), so a sweeper can find them:

```bash
./sweep.sh            # list tagged buckets (dry-run)
./sweep.sh --force    # empty + delete
```

Native `skip_cleanup` + `terraform test cleanup` land as a preview in Terraform 1.16.

## Beyond native testing

Layer the tools, lightest first: static (`tflint`/`trivy`, every commit) → `terraform test` (unit mock + integration apply, this lab) → Terratest when e2e/polling is required. `terraform test` returns an exit code, so stages 1+2 gate a merge in CI without any secret.

lines: -- **conditional encryption**: `count = var.kms_key_arn != null ? 1 : 0` (the decision is driven by the ARN value).

+- **conditional encryption**: `count = local.kms_enabled ? 1 : 0`, where `kms_enabled = var.enable_encryption && var.kms_key_arn != null` (the flag arms it, the ARN is required by the cross-validation).
The root (`main.tf` + `providers.tf`) is an example usage and the configuration under test. Open these first: the point is that a module is decisions you can prove.

attachment.snippet:
1	# Demo run-of-show - live "Terraform test" (2026-06-25).
2	# terraform 1.15.6 (see .terraform-version). The real apply targets the sandbox AWS profile.
3	# Targets are ordered as the progressive demo stages (read the README "Progressive demo").
4	
5	.PHONY: init fmt validate test-validate test-plan test-fast test-deploy test-parallel test sweep clean
6	
7	init:           ## init (providers: aws + random)
8		terraform init
9	
10	fmt:            ## format check
11		terraform fmt -recursive -check -diff
12	
13	validate:       ## validate the config
14		terraform validate
15	
16	test-validate:  ## Stage 1 - variable validations only (expect_failures, creds-free)
17		terraform test -filter=tests/validations.tftest.hcl -verbose
18	
19	test-plan:      ## Stage 2 - mocked plan assertions only (mock_provider, creds-free)
20		terraform test -filter=tests/plan.tftest.hcl -verbose
21	
22	test-fast:      ## Stages 1+2 - creds-free layer (runs in CI without secrets)
23		terraform test -filter=tests/validations.tftest.hcl -filter=tests/plan.tftest.hcl -verbose
24	
25	test-deploy:    ## Stage 3 - integration: real apply on AWS (requires AWS_PROFILE=sandbox)
26		AWS_PROFILE=sandbox terraform test -filter=tests/deploy.tftest.hcl
27	
28	test-parallel:  ## Stage 4 - parallel + state_key: 2 concurrent deployments (AWS_PROFILE=sandbox)
29		AWS_PROFILE=sandbox terraform test -filter=tests/parallel.tftest.hcl
30	
31	test:           ## full suite (AWS_PROFILE=sandbox for the apply stages)
32		AWS_PROFILE=sandbox terraform test
33	
34	sweep:          ## clean up orphaned resources by tag (see sweep.sh)
35		./sweep.sh
36	
37	clean:          ## remove local terraform state
38		find . -type d -name '.terraform' -prune -exec rm -rf {} + ; find . -name '*.tfstate*' -delete

content.text:
Tout est fait et vérifié. Récap :

## Livré (P1 → P2 → P3)

**P1 — Démo progressive**
- `README.md` réécrit en walkthrough staged : table pyramide→diamant (layer / stage / ce qu'il prouve / creds / cible make), Stage 0 (lire le module) → 1 validations → 2 plan mock → 3 apply → 4 parallel, **geste rouge→vert documenté** (Stage 1).
- `Makefile` : cibles atomiques par stage (`test-validate`, `test-plan`) en plus de `test-fast`/`test-deploy`/`test-parallel`/`test`, ordonnées comme la démo.

**P2 — Langue FR→EN** (13 fichiers) : example `AGENTS.md`, 4 `.tftest.hcl`, `main.tf` (root + module), `variables.tf`, `versions.tf`, `providers.tf`, `setup/main.tf`, `sweep.sh` (commentaires **+ strings d'output**). Identifiants déjà EN. Fix précision versions au passage (`state_key` 1.11 / `parallel` 1.12).

**P3 — Conventions (root `AGENTS.md`)**
- Règle **« English everywhere »** explicite (cause racine du dérapage : convention seulement implicite avant).
- Mention des gates CI `commitlint.yml` + `pr-title.yml`.
- `.pre-commit-config.yaml` documenté comme miroir local optionnel des gates (non scaffolé — voir ci-dessous).

## Vérifications
- `terraform fmt -recursive -check` : ✅ clean
- Résidu FR (accents + termes) : ✅ zéro
- `make test-validate` : **5 passed** · `make test-plan` : **4 passed** (couche creds-free verte)

## Restant (décisions, hors P1-P3)
- **D (golden, post-live)** : le gap **S1 reste ouvert** — la CI (`terraform.yml`) ne lance PAS `terraform test`, alors que README + slide affirment « CI gate creds-free ». À câbler post-live (+`-junit-xml`, mini-démo `override_resource`, highlight functions-in-mock 1.15).
- **Pre-commit** : documenté mais pas scaffolé (ajoute une dépendance/outil local). Je crée le `.pre-commit-config.yaml` si tu veux.
- **Commit** : pas committé (GitHub flow = branche + PR ici). Tu veux que je crée la branche + commits conventionnels (`docs(terraform-module-testing): ...`, `refactor(terraform-module-testing): translate to English`, `docs: enforce english + commit gates`) et la PR ?

Veux-tu que je commit/PR, et/ou que je scaffolde le pre-commit ?

content.content:
1	# Repo lessons
2	
3	Durable lessons learned while working in this repository. Append new entries; keep them short and actionable.
4	
5	## CI
6	
7	- **Trivy single-scan + convert** - run `trivy config . --format json --output trivy.json --exit-code 0` once, then drive both the report and the gate from that one JSON with `trivy convert`: `--severity MEDIUM,HIGH,CRITICAL --exit-code 0` for visibility and `--severity HIGH,CRITICAL --exit-code 1` to block. Scanning once and converting twice keeps a single source of truth and avoids re-scanning per severity. Install the CLI with the pinned `aquasecurity/setup-trivy` action (pin the trivy `version:` too) rather than the all-in-one `trivy-action`, so the raw `trivy` commands are available in the step. Pin trivy to a version already proven in CI (e.g. the one `trivy-action` bundles): a brand-new patch can fail to install via setup-trivy's `get.trivy.dev` path before it is mirrored there.
8	
9	## Security hardening
10	
11	- **KMS rotation (`AWS-0065`)** - `trivy config` flags `aws_kms_key` without `enable_key_rotation = true`. Always enable rotation on customer-managed keys; pair it with a deliberately short `deletion_window_in_days` (AWS minimum is 7) only in throwaway demos, with a comment explaining why.
12	- **S3 versioning (`AWS-0090`)** - an encrypted, private bucket still trips the scanner without an `aws_s3_bucket_versioning` resource set to `Enabled`. Versioning is a separate resource, not an inline block on `aws_s3_bucket`.
13	- **Variable validation** - mark required inputs `nullable = false` and add a `validation {}` block (region regex, `cidrhost()` for CIDRs, `contains()` allow-lists for enums). Failures then surface at plan time with a clear message instead of as opaque provider errors at apply.
14	- **Documented exceptions** - low-value scanner findings that would bloat a teaching example (S3 access logging `AWS-0089`, VPC flow logs `AWS-0178`) are documented in the example README rather than silenced with inline ignores.
15	
16	## Dependency management
17	
18	- **Dependabot blind spots** - the `github-actions` ecosystem only updates `uses:` refs (and syncs the `# vX.Y.Z` comment beside a pinned SHA). It cannot bump tool versions held in action *inputs* (`tflint_version`, setup-trivy `version:`) or in `.terraform-version` files.
19	- **Renovate fills them** - Renovate `customManagers` (regex) bump those fields. For inputs, drop a `# renovate: datasource=github-releases depName=<owner/repo>` annotation on the line above the version. For `.terraform-version` (which can't hold a comment), match the file by name and set `datasource`/`depName`/`versioning` in the manager itself. Freeze intentional RC/alpha pins (the `experiment` examples) with a `packageRule` on `matchCurrentValue: "/-(alpha|beta|rc)/"`.
20	- **Validate before merge** - `npx --package renovate renovate-config-validator .github/renovate.json` catches schema errors locally; the Mend app uses the in-repo config (no onboarding PR) once present.
21

file.content:
# Repo lessons

Durable lessons learned while working in this repository. Append new entries; keep them short and actionable.

## CI

- **Trivy single-scan + convert** - run `trivy config . --format json --output trivy.json --exit-code 0` once, then drive both the report and the gate from that one JSON with `trivy convert`: `--severity MEDIUM,HIGH,CRITICAL --exit-code 0` for visibility and `--severity HIGH,CRITICAL --exit-code 1` to block. Scanning once and converting twice keeps a single source of truth and avoids re-scanning per severity. Install the CLI with the pinned `aquasecurity/setup-trivy` action (pin the trivy `version:` too) rather than the all-in-one `trivy-action`, so the raw `trivy` commands are available in the step. Pin trivy to a version already proven in CI (e.g. the one `trivy-action` bundles): a brand-new patch can fail to install via setup-trivy's `get.trivy.dev` path before it is mirrored there.

## Security hardening

- **KMS rotation (`AWS-0065`)** - `trivy config` flags `aws_kms_key` without `enable_key_rotation = true`. Always enable rotation on customer-managed keys; pair it with a deliberately short `deletion_window_in_days` (AWS minimum is 7) only in throwaway demos, with a comment explaining why.
- **S3 versioning (`AWS-0090`)** - an encrypted, private bucket still trips the scanner without an `aws_s3_bucket_versioning` resource set to `Enabled`. Versioning is a separate resource, not an inline block on `aws_s3_bucket`.
- **Variable validation** - mark required inputs `nullable = false` and add a `validation {}` block (region regex, `cidrhost()` for CIDRs, `contains()` allow-lists for enums). Failures then surface at plan time with a clear message instead of as opaque provider errors at apply.
- **Documented exceptions** - low-value scanner findings that would bloat a teaching example (S3 access logging `AWS-0089`, VPC flow logs `AWS-0178`) are documented in the example README rather than silenced with inline ignores.

## Dependency management

- **Dependabot blind spots** - the `github-actions` ecosystem only updates `uses:` refs (and syncs the `# vX.Y.Z` comment beside a pinned SHA). It cannot bump tool versions held in action *inputs* (`tflint_version`, setup-trivy `version:`) or in `.terraform-version` files.
- **Renovate fills them** - Renovate `customManagers` (regex) bump those fields. For inputs, drop a `# renovate: datasource=github-releases depName=<owner/repo>` annotation on the line above the version. For `.terraform-version` (which can't hold a comment), match the file by name and set `datasource`/`depName`/`versioning` in the manager itself. Freeze intentional RC/alpha pins (the `experiment` examples) with a `packageRule` on `matchCurrentValue: "/-(alpha|beta|rc)/"`.
- **Validate before merge** - `npx --package renovate renovate-config-validator .github/renovate.json` catches schema errors locally; the Mend app uses the in-repo config (no onboarding PR) once present.

# Local mirror of the CI quality gates (see .github/workflows/terraform.yml + commitlint.yml).
# Install: pipx install pre-commit && pre-commit install --install-hooks
#          pre-commit install --hook-type commit-msg   # for conventional commits
# Run on everything: pre-commit run --all-files
repos:
  - repo: https://github.com/antonbabenko/pre-commit-terraform
    rev: v1.99.0
    hooks:
      - id: terraform_fmt
      - id: terraform_validate
      - id: terraform_tflint
        args:
          - --args=--recursive
          - --args=--format=compact

  - repo: https://github.com/pre-commit/pre-commit-hooks
    rev: v6.0.0
    hooks:
      - id: end-of-file-fixer
      - id: trailing-whitespace
      - id: check-merge-conflict
      - id: check-yaml
      - id: mixed-line-ending

  - repo: https://github.com/compilerla/conventional-pre-commit
    rev: v4.2.0
    hooks:
      - id: conventional-pre-commit
        stages: [commit-msg]

# AGENTS.md

Guidance for AI coding agents (Claude Code, Cursor, OpenAI Codex, Aider, etc.) working in this repository. A `CLAUDE.md` symlink points here so Claude Code picks it up automatically. Each example also has its own nested `AGENTS.md` with example-specific guidance - read it before editing inside an example.

## What this repo is

A **library of self-contained Terraform examples**. Each lives under `examples/<name>/` and is independent: its own `README.md`, `AGENTS.md`, tooling (`.terraform-version`, `.tflint.hcl`) and Terraform root module(s). Examples are **not** factored into shared code - isolation is intentional so each can be read, run, and live-demoed on its own.

Use cases served: reusable patterns, illustrations for blog/LinkedIn posts, and starting points / labs for live tech sessions.

## Taxonomy

Every example declares a primary `type` plus free-form tags. Source of truth is the catalogue table in the root `README.md`; each example's `README.md`/`AGENTS.md` restates its type.

- `pattern` - reusable, production-leaning reference.
- `lab` - starting point / TP with progressive steps for a live session.
- `experiment` - preview/RC features, may be intentionally unstable.

Do **not** create top-level category directories (`patterns/`, `experiments/`...). Keep `examples/` flat and classify via `type` + tags. This avoids lifecycle churn when an example changes category.

## How to add an example (golden path)

1. `mkdir examples/<name>/` - kebab-case, scope-first (`aws-multi-env`, `terraform-rc-variables`).
2. Add `README.md` (state its `type` + tags, objective, run steps) and `AGENTS.md` (example-specific guidance).
3. Add per-example tooling: `.terraform-version` (pin, may be an RC/prerelease for `experiment` types) and `.tflint.hcl`.
4. Add the Terraform root module(s). Any directory containing a `providers.tf` is auto-discovered by CI - no CI edit needed to validate it.
5. Register a row in the root `README.md` catalogue table.

## CI

`.github/workflows/terraform.yml` auto-discovers Terraform root modules (directories containing `providers.tf`) under `examples/` and runs `validate` + `tflint` + `trivy` per module in a matrix. `terraform fmt -check -recursive` runs once at the root. The `tf-setup` composite action installs Terraform from the target example's `.terraform-version`. Adding an example requires no CI change.

## Repository standards

- **Language**: English everywhere in committed content - code, comments, identifiers, docs, `README.md`, `AGENTS.md`, script output strings. No other language, including inside an example. (This repo is read on screen and public; mixed languages stand out.)
- **Git flow**: GitHub flow (short-lived feature branches off `main`, PR review, squash-merge).
- **Work tracking**: durable backlog = GitHub Issues + Milestone (a goal + its acceptance criteria); each PR references its issue (`Closes #N`). `main` carries no task file - session plans stay local/ephemeral. `tasks/lessons.md` holds durable repo lessons.
- **Commit messages**: [Conventional Commits](https://www.conventionalcommits.org/) - `<type>(<scope>): <subject>`. Use the example name as scope when relevant (e.g. `feat(aws-multi-env): ...`).
  - Common types: `feat`, `fix`, `docs`, `chore`, `refactor`, `test`, `ci`, `build`.
  - Breaking changes: append `!` after type/scope or add a `BREAKING CHANGE:` footer.
  - Keep subject imperative, lowercase, no trailing period, ~72 chars max.
  - Enforced in CI: `commitlint.yml` (commit messages, config-conventional) and `pr-title.yml` (semantic PR title, since squash-merge uses the PR title).
- **Versioning**: [SemVer](https://semver.org/), repo-level single release line via release-please.
- **Validation before commit**: `terraform fmt -recursive` (root) and `terraform validate` inside each touched root module. Optional local mirror of the CI gates (fmt/tflint/validate) via a `.pre-commit-config.yaml` - not yet wired; add it if you want push-time enforcement.

lines: - Keep subject imperative, lowercase, no trailing period, ~72 chars max.
- Enforced in CI: `commitlint.yml` (commit messages, config-conventional) and `pr-title.yml` (semantic PR title, since squash-merge uses the PR title).
- **Versioning**: [SemVer](https://semver.org/), repo-level single release line via release-please.

-- **Validation before commit**: `terraform fmt -recursive` (root) and `terraform validate` inside each touched root module. Optional local mirror of the CI gates (fmt/tflint/validate) via a `.pre-commit-config.yaml` - not yet wired; add it if you want push-time enforcement.

Post-live (2026-06-25) follow-ups from the `terraform test` golden-pass review. The lab covers the golden core (`run` plan/apply, `assert`, `expect_failures`, `mock_provider`, setup module + `run.x.output`, file/run-level `variables`, `parallel`+`state_key`). These are the notable features still uncovered, ranked by value.

## Scope
- [ ] **Gap S1 - wire the creds-free layer into CI** (`.github/workflows/terraform.yml`): run `terraform test -filter=tests/validations.tftest.hcl -filter=tests/plan.tftest.hcl` for this example. Today the README + live slide claim a "CI gate, mock layer runs without secrets" but CI only does validate/tflint/trivy - the tests never run. Add `-junit-xml` report output.
- [ ] **`override_resource` / `mock_resource { defaults }`** mini-demo: show mocking depth beyond a bare `mock_provider "aws" {}` (skip a slow/computed resource).
- [ ] **1.15 highlight**: functions inside mock `defaults` (changelog #34672) - a recent-feature callout.
- [ ] Niche, mention-only: `override_during = plan` (1.11), `plan_options` (refresh-only/replace/target), `*.tfmock.hcl` reusable mock library, `expect_failures` against outputs/checks.
- [ ] 1.16 preview already teased in README (`skip_cleanup` / `terraform test cleanup`) - upgrade from teaser to a runnable note once 1.16 ships.

## Acceptance
- CI runs the creds-free `terraform test` layer per push/PR and uploads a JUnit report; README "CI gate" claim is now true.
- At least one `override_*`/`mock_resource` example present and green.
- CI green; `make test-fast` still passes.

## Context
- Live piece: `02-Areas/Éditorial/Pieces/2026-06-24 Live - Terraform test.md` (anchor S1 "CI gate").
- Branch `feat/terraform-module-testing`, follows the staged-demo + English-translation work.
EOF
)" 2>&1 | tail -3

content.text:
Parce que l'`AGENTS.md` du repo définit le backlog durable comme **GitHub Issues + Milestone** (« durable backlog = GitHub Issues »), et aucune issue n'est ouverte → côté *code*, les items Golden ne sont tracés nulle part. J'allais donc créer l'issue D pour les poser au bon endroit *selon la convention du repo*.

Mais « en mode hand-off » me fait penser que tu vises plutôt le **handoff tracker du live** (côté vault — commit `project(bga-live): handoff tracker ... items P1/P2`), pas une issue GitHub. Ce sont deux endroits différents :

- **GitHub Issue** = backlog technique du repo (convention `AGENTS.md`).
- **Handoff tracker (vault)** = reprise du live, où vivent déjà les P1/P2.

Je n'ai rien créé. Où veux-tu que je trace les items Golden (D + gap S1) ?
